findings.message: Destructive irreversible action with no confirmation: clicking Delete immediately issues a DELETE and reloads the page. A misclick removes the item with no chance to cancel or undo.
findings.suggestion: Gate the delete behind a confirmation step (e.g. window.confirm, a confirm dialog, or a two-step 'Delete -> Confirm' affordance), and/or provide an undo toast after deletion.

findings.message: Mutating action has no in-flight feedback: the button is not disabled while the DELETE request is pending, so users can double-click and fire duplicate requests with no visible 'deleting...' state.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state (e.g. useState<boolean>) and set the button to disabled with a 'Deleting...' label while the request is in flight.

findings.message: No error feedback on failure: the fetch result is not checked for response.ok and no catch branch surfaces an error to the user. On a 4xx/5xx or network failure the page silently reloads as if the delete succeeded.
findings.suggestion: Check response.ok / wrap in try-catch and render a visible error message (e.g. a role=alert) instead of unconditionally reloading.

findings.message: Full-page window.location.reload() after a single-item delete is a heavy, disorienting feedback pattern: it scrolls to top, loses focus, and discards any client state, giving no localized success indication.
findings.suggestion: Update the items list in state (optimistically remove the row or refetch the list) and show a brief success toast, instead of reloading the whole page.
